summary: Russian forces reported the liberation of Konstantinovka and Yurkovka, claiming control over hundreds of buildings and significant Ukrainian losses. UAV activity continues across multiple fronts, including attacks on civilian infrastructure in Kherson, Kharkiv, and the Black Sea. Russian drone strikes also targeted fuel storage and transport assets in the Odessa region.
key_points: Russian troops liberated Konstantinovka, occupying 627 buildings and reporting over 540 Ukrainian casualties and loss of equipment (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31026).
UAVs were observed over Zaporizhzhia from the left bank toward Khortytsia and from the south heading into the city (Source: @eRadarrua/97293; @kpszsu/65606).
A Russian drone attacked a minibus in Kherson's Korabelny district, injuring at least four civilians (Source: @UkraineNow/82043).
Russian forces struck a fuel tanker parking lot in southern Odessa region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31017).
A controlled Shahed missile launched from Odesa crossed into Moldova (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67909).
Russian UAVs bombed Kharkiv with a guided bomb, causing nine injuries, including four children, and damaging over forty houses (Source: @UkraineNow/82042).
Drone attacks on civilian ships in the Black Sea resulted in one fatality and multiple injuries (Source: @UkraineNow/82044).
Air defenses over Crimea, Sevastopol, Melitopol and other regions shot down two UAVs in Sevastopol (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31016).

sections.executive_summary: Russian forces announced the liberation of Konstantinovka, reporting control of 627 buildings and heavy Ukrainian losses. UAV activity remains intense across multiple fronts, striking civilian transport, fuel infrastructure and maritime targets. Air defense systems continue to engage drones over Crimea, Sevastopol and other regions.

points: Ukrainian authorities reported a UAV over Zaporizhzhia moving from the left bank toward Khortytsia, indicating continued aerial threats to the city (Source: @eRadarrua/97293).
In Kharkiv, a guided bomb attack injured nine civilians, including four children, and damaged more than forty private homes (Source: @UkraineNow/82042).
Ukrainian civilian shipping in the Black Sea suffered a drone strike that killed a crew member on a Panamanian vessel and injured crew on two other ships (Source: @UkraineNow/82044).
Two UAVs were shot down by air defenses in the northern sector of Sevastopol, reflecting active aerial combat in the region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31016).
Ukrainian forces reported attacks on Nova Poshta terminals in Sumy and Zaporizhzhia, highlighting disruption of logistics (Source: @eRadarrua/97289).
Russian troops claimed liberation of Konstantinovka, occupying 627 buildings and reporting over 540 Ukrainian casualties, 160 weapons and 116 vehicles lost (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31026).
The Russian Ministry of Defense announced the capture of Yurkovka in the DPR (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31025).
Russian forces struck a fuel tanker parking lot in the southern Odessa region, targeting logistical assets (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31017).
A Russian-controlled Shahed missile launched from Odesa crossed into Moldovan airspace, indicating cross‑border missile use (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67909).
Russian UAVs conducted multiple strikes across the front, including attacks on civilian transport in Kherson and bombing runs in Kharkiv (Source: @UkraineNow/82043; @UkraineNow/82042).

In Konstantinovka, Russian assault groups eliminated surrounded Ukrainian units in the southwestern part of the settlement and advanced in the Nikolaevsky microdistrict (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31026).
Russian forces seized 37 strongholds and 251 buildings in Krasy Liman, reporting 185 Ukrainian casualties and loss of 45 weapons (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31026).
UAV activity was reported west of Kharkiv heading toward Lyubotyn, suggesting reconnaissance or strike missions (Source: @kpszsu/65579).
A group of UAVs operated east of Kharkiv near Savintsi, moving westward, indicating pressure on the eastern approaches (Source: @kpszsu/65619).
UAVs from Sumy moved toward Poltava and Gadyach, showing cross‑regional aerial operations (Source: @kpszsu/65634).
UAVs were observed in the north‑west of Kyiv region on a south‑west course, posing a threat to the capital's outskirts (Source: @kpszsu/65579).
A UAV was reported in the Novgorod‑Siverskyi area of Chernihiv region heading south (Source: @kpszsu/65600).
UAVs from Chernihiv region moved toward the Vyshhorod district of Kyiv region (Source: @kpszsu/65577).
Reactive UAVs in southern Sumy were directed toward Okhtyrka, indicating potential strike intent (Source: @kpszsu/65609).
Air defense over Sevastopol shot down two UAVs, reflecting ongoing aerial engagements in the north of the Crimean peninsula (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31016).
Russian UAVs attacked a minibus in Kherson's Korabelny district, injuring at least four civilians (Source: @UkraineNow/82043).
A Russian drone strike hit a fuel tanker parking lot in the southern Odessa region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31017).
UAVs were reported in Dnipropetrovsk heading toward Apostolove from the southeast (Source: @kpszsu/65598).
UAVs operated south of Mykolaiv region moving northward, indicating activity near the Black Sea coast (Source: @kpszsu/65630).
UAVs near Snihurivka in Mykolaiv region were observed, suggesting pressure on southern logistics hubs (Source: @kpszsu/65624).
Air defense systems were active over Rostov, Crimea, Sevastopol, Melitopol and Kirillovka, engaging multiple aerial threats (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31016).
Two UAVs were shot down in the northern sector of Sevastopol, confirming Russian aerial activity in the area (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31016).
Russian forces destroyed the Ukrainian "Baba Yaga" drone and its operator, indicating successful counter‑UAV operations (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31022).
UAV attacks on civilian infrastructure continued across multiple regions, including Kherson, Kharkiv, and the Black Sea (Source: @UkraineNow/82043; @UkraineNow/82042; @UkraineNow/82044).
Russian UAVs were observed on various courses across the front lines, from north‑west Kyiv to south‑west Mykolaiv, reflecting widespread aerial deployment (Source: @kpszsu/65579; @kpszsu/65630).

key_developments: Russian troops reported the liberation of Konstantinovka, controlling 627 buildings and claiming over 540 Ukrainian casualties and loss of 160 weapons and 116 vehicles (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31026).

UAV activity intensified across all fronts, with attacks on civilian transport in Kherson, a guided bomb strike in Kharkiv injuring nine, and drone strikes on fuel infrastructure in Odessa (Sources: @UkraineNow/82043; @UkraineNow/82042; @ukr_leaks_eng/31017).
Air defenses over Crimea, Sevastopol, Melitopol and other regions engaged multiple UAVs, shooting down two in Sevastopol's northern side (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31016).
A Russian-controlled Shahed missile launched from Odesa entered Moldovan airspace, raising concerns of cross‑border missile use (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67909).
Russian forces claimed capture of Yurkovka in the DPR, expanding territorial control (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31025).
Drone attacks on civilian ships in the Black Sea resulted in one fatality and several injuries, highlighting maritime vulnerability (Source: @UkraineNow/82044).
Ukrainian forces reported attacks on Nova Poshta logistics terminals in Sumy and Zaporizhzhia, disrupting supply chains (Source: @eRadarrua/97289).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian offensive operations continue to focus on territorial gains in the east, exemplified by the reported liberation of Konstantinovka and Yurkovka, while inflicting substantial Ukrainian personnel and equipment losses. UAV and drone deployments are pervasive, targeting civilian infrastructure, logistics hubs, and maritime assets, thereby extending the conflict's impact beyond front‑line combat. Air defense engagements remain active, but the frequency of drone incursions suggests a growing aerial threat that could strain Ukrainian air defense resources.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian authorities are reinforcing air defense coverage over critical regions such as Crimea, Sevastopol, and the southern oblasts, while also attempting to protect civilian transport and logistics networks. Local administrations in Kherson and Kharkiv have reported civilian casualties and infrastructure damage, prompting emergency response and medical assistance. International observers note the cross‑border missile trajectory into Moldova as a potential escalation point requiring diplomatic attention.
sections.intelligence_notes: Continued monitoring of UAV flight paths is essential, as patterns indicate coordinated strikes across multiple fronts, including north‑west Kyiv, southern Mykolaiv, and eastern Kharkiv. The reported destruction of the Ukrainian "Baba Yaga" drone suggests effective Russian electronic warfare capabilities. The targeting of fuel storage in Odessa and civilian maritime vessels underscores a strategic aim to disrupt Ukraine's logistical and economic lifelines.
